title: Kalighat Kali Temple
tagline: Kalighat Kali Temple — spiritual town of West Bengal

overview.short: Kalighat Kali Temple is a Hindu temple in Kalighat, Kolkata, West Bengal, India, dedicated to the Hindu goddess Kali, one of the 10 Mahavidyas in the Hindu tantric tradition and th
overview.description: Kalighat Kali Temple is a Hindu temple in Kalighat, Kolkata, West Bengal, India, dedicated to the Hindu goddess Kali, one of the 10 Mahavidyas in the Hindu tantric tradition and the supreme deity in the Kalikula worship tradition. The temple is one of the 51 Shakti Pithas in India. According to the Devi Bhagavata Purana, Kalika Purana and Shakti Peetha Stotram, the toes of the right foot of Goddess Sati fell here, after Lord Vishnu's Sudarshan Chakra splintered her body into many parts to calm down Mahadev's rage during his cosmic dance. One of the oldest and most important places of worship i

topPlaces.name: Pathrakaliamman Temple
topPlaces.description: Arulmiku Sri Pathirakaliamman temple is one of the antique temples in Tamil Nadu. It is in Puthiamputhur (Puthimbatore), Tuticorin district.

topPlaces.name: Kalighat Home for the Dying
topPlaces.description: Kalighat, the Home of the Pure Heart (Nirmal Hriday) (formerly Mother Teresa's Kalighat Home for the Dying Destitutes) is a hospice for the sick, destitute and the dying established by St. Mother Teresa in Kalighat, Kolkata, India.

topPlaces.name: Kalighat
topPlaces.description: Kalighat is a locality of South Kolkata, in Kolkata district, West Bengal, India. One of the oldest neighbourhoods in South Kolkata, Kalighat is also densely populated with a history of cultural intermingling with the various foreign incursions into the area over time.

topPlaces.name: Chetla
topPlaces.description: Chetla is a neighbourhood of South Kolkata in Kolkata district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Basusree Cinema, Kolkata
topPlaces.description: Basusree Cinema is a single screen cinema hall located on Shyama Prasad Mukherjee Road, near Jatin Das Park metro station in Kolkata, West Bengal, India. The cinema hall was inaugurated on 19 December 1947 by Satyabhushan Basu and is regarded as one of the city’s heritage theatres.

topPlaces.name: Kalighat metro station
topPlaces.description: Kalighat is an underground metro station on the North-South corridor of the Blue Line of Kolkata Metro at Rashbehari Avenue in Kalighat, Kolkata, West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Jatin Das Park metro station
topPlaces.description: Jatin Das Park is an underground metro station on the North-South corridor of the Blue Line of Kolkata Metro at Hazra, Kalighat, Kolkata, West Bengal, India. It is named after Indian independence activist and revolutionary Jatindra Nath Das.

items.note: Arulmiku Sri Pathirakaliamman temple is one of the antique temples in Tamil Nadu. It is in Puthiamputhur (Puthimbatore), Tuticorin district.

Kalighat, the Home of the Pure Heart (Nirmal Hriday) (formerly Mother Teresa's Kalighat Home for the Dying Destitutes) is a hospice for the sick, destitute and the dying established by St. Mother Teresa in Kalighat, Kolkata, India.

Kalighat is a locality of South Kolkata, in Kolkata district, West Bengal, India. One of the oldest neighbourhoods in South Kolkata, Kalighat is also densely populated with a history of cultural intermingling with the various foreign incursions into the area over time.
Chetla is a neighbourhood of South Kolkata in Kolkata district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

Basusree Cinema is a single screen cinema hall located on Shyama Prasad Mukherjee Road, near Jatin Das Park metro station in Kolkata, West Bengal, India. The cinema hall was inaugurated on 19 December 1947 by Satyabhushan Basu and is regarded as one of the city’s heritage theatres.
Kalighat is an underground metro station on the North-South corridor of the Blue Line of Kolkata Metro at Rashbehari Avenue in Kalighat, Kolkata, West Bengal, India.

itinerary.title: Day 3 — Jatin Das Park metro station

items.note: Jatin Das Park is an underground metro station on the North-South corridor of the Blue Line of Kolkata Metro at Hazra, Kalighat, Kolkata, West Bengal, India. It is named after Indian independence activist and revolutionary Jatindra Nath Das.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kalighat Kali Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kalighat Kali Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kalighat Kali Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (18 km) and the nearest railway station is Howrah Junction (Kolkata) (7 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kalighat Kali Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kalighat Kali Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kalighat Kali Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kalighat Kali Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kalighat Kali Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Pathrakaliamman Temple, Kalighat Home for the Dying, Kalighat, Chetla, Basusree Cinema, Kolkata.
